metric_value: Three fundamental strategies: deliver industry-leading value; drive standards; move high-end functionality downstream to workgroup
notes: Application of SCM principles to storage market

metric_value: Continuous price/performance improvement via superior SCM techniques passed along to customers as sustainable competitive advantage
notes: Built into Dell's DNA per report

metric_value: Standards-based virtual feedback loop: standardization enables new users driving market growth; growth drives technology refinement and cost reduction
notes: Dell participating in FCLC; Serial ATA II; Serial Attached SCSI; SNIA

metric_value: Dell positions as storage integrator (not OEM) to simplify selection of disk arrays; tape; and storage software for customers
notes: Provides operational flexibility and agility to react to change

metric_value: Dell lives and breathes price elasticity: lower cost (customer benefit) yields higher volume (Dell benefit); drives faster storage price reductions than industry norm
notes: Aberdeen Conclusions section

metric_value: 60% of Aberdeen survey respondents cite managing storage within current budget/headcount as critical success factor
notes: Recent Aberdeen survey data

metric_value: TCO and ROI remain highly popular components of financial discipline for justifying storage capital investments
notes: Aberdeen survey finding

metric_value: Obtaining satisfactory performance at less cost is emerging as a major buyer trend; midline ATA storage products satisfying this demand
notes: Aberdeen research on enterprise storage purchasing trends

metric_value: 70% faster than CX200 predecessor at same price point
notes: Third-generation workgroup-focused array

metric_value: 100% faster than CX400 predecessor at same price point
notes: Third-generation department-focused array

metric_value: 25% faster than CX600 predecessor at same price point
notes: Third-generation data center-focused array

metric_value: Controller-only upgrade from CX200 to CX300; no data migration needed; customer pays only for new disk controller not whole new array
notes: Third-generation demonstrates partnership's ability to deliver investment protection

metric_value: Dell-conceived product for easier FC at lower price than CX; scales to 3TB on SATA; wizard-based setup; includes all management and backup software
notes: New Brocade switch offered by Dell for AX100 SAN connectivity

metric_value: SnapView point-in-time copy available across entire CX line (CX300 through CX700) and AX100; CX200 did not have this capability
notes: Demonstrates moving high-end functionality downstream to workgroup

metric_value: Dell|EMC arrays support mixed ATA and FC drives; FC drives house FLARE OS; ATA drives for lower-cost point-in-time copies
notes: Enables tiered storage within single array

metric_value: 775N and 770N midrange central file servers; 745N entry-level; all use Windows Storage Server 2003; support CIFS/AFP/NFS
notes: Midrange models connect to Dell|EMC FC arrays and can gateway to SAN via FC switch

metric_value: Windows Storage Server 2003 eliminates learning curve vs proprietary NAS OS; inherits Advanced Server features including Active Directory and clustering
notes: Illustrates Dell's standards commitment

metric_value: PowerVault 160T enterprise library uses only LTO-2 drives; Dell likely to emphasize LTO on other tape libraries in future
notes: DLT and LTO supported on 122T/132T/136T; LTO-only on enterprise 160T

metric_value: Multi-vendor software: Navisphere device management; SnapView/MirrorView replication; Legato Networker; VisualSAN management; CommVault Galaxy; VERITAS BackupExec/NetBackup
notes: Dell partners with multiple software vendors to cover broad storage management needs

metric_value: Bundling reduces SKU complexity and deployment friction; ensures all software components included; makes complex solutions available to broader audiences
notes: Dell views bundling through lens of reducing purchasing friction and complexity

metric_value: Minimizing number of management consoles reduces context switching; Dell drives vendors to single-console integration via compelling business opportunity
notes: Working with standards organizations to define common API and CLI for storage/server console integration

metric_value: Dell knows location of all storage systems due to direct sales; enables targeted preventive maintenance; remote management via WebEx
notes: Enterprise Command Center for electronic monitoring and proactive problem prevention

metric_value: Structured multi-vendor accountability with Dell Service agreements covering execution guidelines; SLAs; escalation policies; global performance metrics
notes: Dell notified of partner-identified problems in real time

metric_value: Historical incident database enables preventive maintenance; root cause analysis for each problem; Dell can spot problems before they affect customers
notes: Cost-effective approach to improving efficiency and customer satisfaction

metric_value: Dell|EMC partnership extended from 5-year to 7-year agreement through Dec 2008; demonstrates long-term value both companies derive from relationship
notes: Implicit prediction: partnership will continue to deliver value beyond 2008

metric_value: Dell acquired EMC for $67 billion in 2016 forming Dell Technologies; partnership far outlasted 2008 extension and culminated in largest tech acquisition in history
notes: Partnership deepened continuously from co-branding to full acquisition

metric_value: Dell's standards-based approach (SATA; SAS; FC standards) predicted to drive improved technology and reduced costs via virtual feedback loop
notes: Dell making big push to support IT-based standards in storage

metric_value: Standards prevailed: SATA became dominant capacity-tier interface; SAS became standard enterprise performance interface; proprietary interfaces declined industry-wide
notes: Open standards drove cost reductions exactly as Dell's feedback loop model predicted

metric_value: Aberdeen concludes Dell's storage team deserves place at business discussion table for companies of all sizes and government agencies after five years of product line growth
notes: Dell leveraging SMB success to address larger enterprise storage needs

metric_value: Dell became #1 in enterprise external storage market post-EMC acquisition; Dell Technologies consistently led worldwide external storage revenue share
notes: Dell moved from aspiring enterprise storage player to undisputed market leader

metric_value: Dell proactively drives faster price/performance improvements; competitors reactively pass on technology improvements
notes: Many other storage suppliers reactive; Dell proactive

metric_value: Dell's standards orientation makes it simultaneously easier and less costly to integrate Dell storage into heterogeneous environments; lowers proprietary lock-in risk
notes: Customer choice through lower friction of integration

metric_value: Dell can move as rapidly as any supplier to make storage products less expensive; better; faster across Dell|EMC; PowerVault NAS; and PowerVault tape products
notes: Storage buyers seeking TCO/ROI value should consider Dell solutions

metric_value: EMC learned from Dell how SMB segment benefits from enterprise-class storage; CX200/CX300/AX100 developed through Dell's workgroup insights
notes: Bidirectional knowledge transfer in Dell|EMC partnership

metric_value: CX700 for data center; CX500 for department; CX300 for workgroup; AX100 for entry-level; PowerVault NAS for file serving; PowerVault tape for data protection
notes: Comprehensive product range reaching from data center to workgroup
